message:
Improve search query parsing and Tantivy integration

This commit enhances the search subsystem with better query parsing,
more sophisticated Tantivy schema configuration, and improved result
ranking to provide more relevant search results across conversation history.

Query parsing improvements (query.rs):
- Enhanced QueryParser with support for multiple query syntaxes:
  - Phrase queries: "exact match" in double quotes
  - Boolean operators: AND, OR, NOT with precedence handling
  - Field-specific queries: agent:codex, workspace:/path/to/project
  - Wildcard patterns: migrat* for prefix matching
  - Fuzzy matching: ~similarity for typo tolerance
- Query sanitization to prevent injection of malformed Tantivy syntax
- Fallback to term-based search when complex query parsing fails
- Empty query handling with graceful degradation
- Query transformation for FTS5 compatibility (SQLite backend)

Tantivy schema and indexing enhancements (tantivy.rs):
- Refined schema definition with optimized field configurations:
  - message_content: TEXT field with full tokenization and position tracking
  - conversation_title: TEXT field with phrase matching support
  - agent_slug: STRING field for exact filtering (not tokenized)
  - workspace_path: STRING field with path-aware tokenization
  - created_at: DATE field for temporal range queries
  - conversation_id: stored field for result correlation
- Index settings tuned for conversation search workload:
  - 50MB heap size for writer (balances memory and throughput)
  - Commit on close to ensure durability
  - Optimized tokenizer pipeline: lowercase + stemming + stop words
  - Position tracking enabled for phrase and proximity queries

Scoring and ranking improvements:
- BM25 ranking with boosted fields (title weighted 2x content)
- Multi-field query expansion (search both title and content)
- Recency bias: recent conversations slightly boosted in results
- Result deduplication by conversation_id (show best message per conversation)
- Snippet extraction with highlighted match context (100 char window)

Performance optimizations:
- Lazy index loading (mmap-based directory)
- Query result caching for repeated searches
- Early termination for top-k queries (limit respected)
- Incremental index updates (no full rebuild on new conversations)

Integration with dual backend:
- Tantivy backend: used for complex queries (phrase, fuzzy, boolean)
- SQLite FTS5 backend: used for simple keyword searches (faster cold start)
- Query router automatically selects optimal backend based on query complexity
- Fallback chain: Tantivy → FTS5 → full table scan if both fail

Error handling:
- Graceful degradation on index corruption (falls back to SQLite)
- Query syntax error messages with suggestions
- Empty result handling with search hints (try fewer filters, broader terms)

Testing considerations:
- Query parser tested against common search patterns
- Schema verified against realistic conversation data from all six connectors
- Ranking validated manually against sample queries

This completes the dual-backend search infrastructure introduced in the
commit "Implement dual-backend SearchClient supporting Tantivy and SQLite
FTS5" by providing the query parsing and schema configuration layer.
